title: Choosing a density estimator
text: "linear_gaussian" — closed-form conditional Gaussian fit linear regression. neural network torch. exact true posterior Gaussian parameters, makes fast baseline sanity check neural estimators. "mdn" — mixture density network: small neural network maps x weights, means, covariances Gaussian mixture. Mixtures can represent multimodal skewed posteriors, training quick. "maf" (default) — masked autoregressive flow (Papamakarios et al., 2017): builds posterior density sequence invertible transformations Gaussian. flexible mixture parameters strong nonlinear dependence. also default Python sbi. "nsf" — neural spline flow (Durkan et al., 2019): flow whose transformations monotonic splines. flexible three, somewhat higher training cost. three neural estimators need torch back end (install.packages("torch"); torch::install_torch()) share one training loop, training arguments npe() — max_epochs, patience, n_restarts, — mean thing whichever pick.

what: A posterior no Gaussian can fit
title: Choosing a density estimator
text: two-moons benchmark task bimodal, crescent-shaped posterior. built package: linear-Gaussian estimator represent two modes; returns single wide Gaussian averages across : plot chunk unnamed-chunk-3 MDN, mixture components, recovers moons: plot chunk unnamed-chunk-4 spline flow captures sharp crescent edges cleanly: plot chunk unnamed-chunk-5
code: library(neuralsbi) #>  #> Attaching package: 'neuralsbi' #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     sample  task <- task_two_moons() task #> <nsbi_task> two_moons: 2 parameters -> 2 data dims  x_obs <- c(0, 0) fit_lg <- npe(task$prior, task$simulator, n_simulations = 2000,               density_estimator = "linear_gaussian", seed = 1) draws_lg <- sample(posterior(fit_lg, x_obs = x_obs), 3000) pairplot(draws_lg) fit_mdn <- npe(task$prior, task$simulator, n_simulations = 2000,                density_estimator = "mdn", n_components = 10,                max_epochs = 200, seed = 1) draws_mdn <- sample(posterior(fit_mdn, x_obs = x_obs), 3000) pairplot(draws_mdn) fit_nsf <- npe(task$prior, task$simulator, n_simulations = 2000,                density_estimator = "nsf", max_epochs = 150, seed = 1) draws_nsf <- sample(posterior(fit_nsf, x_obs = x_obs), 3000) pairplot(draws_nsf)

title: Choosing a density estimator
text: classifier two-sample test (c2st()) measures distinguishable two sets samples : 0.5 means classifier tell apart, 1.0 means always can. MDN flow agree closely — recover two moons, classifier near chance: linear-Gaussian fit visibly wrong — one blob instead two crescents — yet c2st() flag : number trap worth understanding. c2st() trains linear classifier, separates samples mean covariance alone. two moons symmetric origin — centre roughly spread Gaussian blob — straight decision boundary can tell two sets apart, even though one bimodal . pairplots show exactly difference score misses. lesson: two-sample score sharp classifier, linear one blind multimodality. , trust picture, calibration check (vignette("diagnostics")). two posteriors differ mean covariance — estimated exact posterior task_gaussian_linear() — linear classifier detects gap well, c2st() right tool. package’s accuracy tests score estimators analytic references.
code: c2st(draws_mdn, draws_nsf, seed = 1)$accuracy   # near 0.5: MDN and NSF agree #> [1] 0.5091667 c2st(draws_lg, draws_nsf, seed = 1)$accuracy    # also near 0.5 (see below) #> [1] 0.5121667

title: Choosing a density estimator
text: estimator settings, passed npe(), control flexible : flexible model helps enough simulations train ; thousand simulations defaults usually right. estimated posterior looks smooth wide, adding simulations usually helps enlarging network.

title: Choosing a density estimator
text: default "maf" matches Python sbi handles strong nonlinear dependence parameters. "mdn" trains little faster can represent multiple modes. posterior sharp features MAF MDN smooth , switch "nsf". Use "linear_gaussian" torch unavailable, want near-instant baseline, model really linear-Gaussian — exact. Whatever choose, check . vignette("diagnostics") shows verify fitted posterior calibration predictive checks.

title: Checking the posterior
text: Simulation-based calibration (SBC) checks posterior average prior, reference posterior needed. idea: draw theta prior, simulate x, rank true theta among posterior draws given x. posterior calibrated, ranks uniform. plot chunk unnamed-chunk-3 Read rank histogram like : Flat — calibrated. U-shaped — posterior narrow (overconfident). common failure; usually fixed simulations longer training. Hump-shaped — wide (underconfident). Sloped — systematically biased one direction.
code: res <- sbc(fit, task$simulator, n_sbc = 100, n_posterior_samples = 200,            seed = 2) res #> <nsbi_sbc> 100 trials, 200 posterior samples each #>   per-parameter uniformity p-values (large = calibrated): #>     0.444  0.470  0.684  0.326  0.970  0.083  0.684  0.025  0.444  0.735  plot_sbc(res, param = 1)

title: Checking the posterior
text: SBC ranks yield coverage curve: often p% credible interval actually contain truth? plot chunk unnamed-chunk-4 calibrated posterior hugs diagonal. Points diagonal mean intervals narrow — 90% interval covers truth 70% time.

title: Checking the posterior
text: SBC checks parameter’s marginal. TARP (Lemos et al., 2023) tests coverage joint posterior using random reference points, can detect miscalibration per-parameter ranks miss. plot chunk unnamed-chunk-5
code: tr <- tarp(fit, task$simulator, n_tarp = 100, n_posterior_samples = 200,            seed = 3) tr #> <nsbi_tarp> 100 trials, 200 posterior samples each #>   max |ECP - nominal|: 0.130 (0 = perfectly calibrated) #>   plot with plot_tarp() plot_tarp(tr)   # ECP curve on the diagonal = calibrated

title: Checking the posterior
text: Calibration average many simulated datasets; predictive check asks observation actually . Push posterior draws back simulator see whether observed data look typical simulations produce. plot chunk unnamed-chunk-6 observation sits tails predictive distribution, either posterior fit poor x simulator reproduce data — model-misspecification signal calibration check provides.
code: theta_true <- sample_prior(task$prior, 1) x_obs <- task$simulator(theta_true)  post <- posterior(fit, x_obs = x_obs) pred <- posterior_predictive(post, task$simulator, n = 500) plot_posterior_predictive(pred, x_obs)

title: Checking the posterior
text: reference posterior exists — analytic, MCMC, long-run fit trust — classifier two-sample test gives single accuracy score. classifier distinguish estimated reference draws (accuracy near 0.5) means posteriors match. rule thumb, accuracies 0.55–0.6 indicate close match; values near 1.0 mean estimated posterior badly .
code: draws     <- sample(post, 5000) reference <- task$reference_posterior(x_obs, n = 5000)  c2st(draws, reference)$accuracy   # ~0.5: indistinguishable from the exact posterior #> [1] 0.5519

title: Checking the posterior
text: every fit, run sbc() look plot_sbc() plot_coverage(). cheap relative training catches overconfidence early. using posterior real conclusions, run posterior predictive check actual observation. change estimator training settings, compare old new draws c2st() see whether change moved posterior . case study vignette("sir-epidemic") applies routine end end applied problem.

title: Getting started with neuralsbi
text: Every SBI problem needs (1) prior, (2) simulator, (3) observation. small linear-Gaussian model whose posterior happen know closed form, can check answer.
code: library(neuralsbi)  # (1) prior over two parameters prior <- prior_normal(mean = c(0, 0), sd = 1)  # (2) simulator: an (n x 2) matrix of parameters -> an (n x 2) matrix of data sigma <- 0.5 simulator <- function(theta) {   theta + matrix(rnorm(length(theta), sd = sigma), nrow = nrow(theta)) }  # (3) the observation we want to explain x_obs <- c(1.0, -0.5)

title: Getting started with neuralsbi
text: npe() draws parameters prior, runs simulator, trains conditional density estimator. linear-Gaussian model, use closed-form conditional-Gaussian estimator, exact requires neural network training. fitted network approximates posterior observation, just one hand — “amortized” means. train , condition new data without refitting. plot chunk unnamed-chunk-4
code: fit <- npe(prior, simulator, n_simulations = 2000,            density_estimator = "linear_gaussian", seed = 1) fit #> <nsbi_npe> Neural Posterior Estimation fit #>   density estimator : linear_gaussian #>   parameters (dim)  : 2 #>   data (dim)        : 2 #>   simulations       : 2000 #>   -> build a posterior with posterior(fit, x_obs = ...) post  <- posterior(fit, x_obs = x_obs) draws <- sample(post, 2000)  colMeans(draws)          # posterior mean #> [1]  0.8043282 -0.3931149 map_estimate(post)       # MAP point estimate #> [1]  0.8071431 -0.3851586 pairplot(draws)          # joint + marginal view

what: Did it work? Check against the truth
title: Getting started with neuralsbi
text: model posterior known Gaussian. Let us compare.
code: d <- 2 Sigma <- solve(diag(d) + diag(d) / sigma^2) mu    <- as.numeric(Sigma %*% (x_obs / sigma^2))  rbind(analytic = mu, estimated = colMeans(draws)) #>                [,1]       [,2] #> analytic  0.8000000 -0.4000000 #> estimated 0.8043282 -0.3931149  # classifier two-sample test: ~0.5 => our samples look like analytic samples z <- matrix(rnorm(2000 * d), ncol = d) analytic_draws <- sweep(z %*% chol(Sigma), 2, mu, `+`) c2st(draws, analytic_draws)$accuracy #> [1] 0.50425

what: Calibration when you don’t know the truth
title: Getting started with neuralsbi
text: Usually analytic posterior. Simulation-based calibration (SBC) still tells whether posterior well calibrated: produce uniform rank statistics. plot chunk unnamed-chunk-6

title: Getting started with neuralsbi
text: non-Gaussian posteriors, need neural density estimator like Mixture Density Network (MDN). requires torch.
code: fit_mdn <- npe(prior, simulator, n_simulations = 2000,                density_estimator = "mdn", max_epochs = 200, seed = 1) fit_mdn #> <nsbi_npe> Neural Posterior Estimation fit #>   density estimator : mdn #>   parameters (dim)  : 2 #>   data (dim)        : 2 #>   simulations       : 2000 #>   best val loss     : 1.2668 #>   -> build a posterior with posterior(fit, x_obs = ...)  # on this Gaussian model the MDN matches the exact linear_gaussian posterior draws_mdn <- sample(posterior(fit_mdn, x_obs = x_obs), 2000) rbind(analytic = mu, mdn = colMeans(draws_mdn)) #>               [,1]      [,2] #> analytic 0.8000000 -0.400000 #> mdn      0.8304641 -0.368187 c2st(draws_mdn, analytic_draws)$accuracy #> [1] 0.526

title: Getting started with neuralsbi
text: MDN limited Gaussian posteriors: recovers bimodal, crescent-shaped posterior classic two-moons task, flow estimators ("maf", "nsf") go still. comparison subject vignette("density-estimators").

title: Getting started with neuralsbi
text: vignettes build : vignette("density-estimators") — estimator use, . vignette("diagnostics") — calibration predictive checks fitted posterior. vignette("sir-epidemic") — complete Bayesian workflow applied epidemic-model calibration. ?npe, ?posterior, ?sbc document every argument.

title: Case study: inferring epidemic parameters (SIR)
text: classic SIR model splits population size N Susceptible, Infected, Recovered compartments. Two rates govern dynamics: contact rate β\beta (fast infection spreads) recovery rate γ\gamma (fast infected individuals recover). package includes model built-task. task bundles prior (log-normal β,γ\beta, \gamma) simulator solves SIR dynamics returns observed infected fraction 10 time points.
code: library(neuralsbi)  task <- task_sir()          # prior + simulator for the SIR model task #> <nsbi_task> sir: 2 parameters -> 10 data dims

title: Case study: inferring epidemic parameters (SIR)
text: simulate prior train neural posterior estimator. Mixture Density Network good default ; posterior smooth unimodal. Training amortized: one fit can conditioned incidence curve without re-simulating.
code: fit <- npe(task$prior, task$simulator,            n_simulations = 3000,            density_estimator = "mdn",            max_epochs = 250, seed = 1)

title: Case study: inferring epidemic parameters (SIR)
text: Suppose observe outbreak generated β=0.4,γ=0.125\beta = 0.4,\ \gamma = 0.125 (basic reproduction number R0=β/γ=3.2R_0 = \beta/\gamma = 3.2). plot chunk unnamed-chunk-4 posterior concentrates around true rates, — importantly — reports uncertainty.
code: theta_true <- c(beta = 0.4, gamma = 0.125) x_obs <- task$simulator(matrix(theta_true, nrow = 1))  post <- posterior(fit, x_obs = x_obs) summary(post, n = 5000) #>   parameter      mean          sd      q2.5       q25       q50       q75 #> 1    theta1 0.4007750 0.006536198 0.3897076 0.3967231 0.4005885 0.4044068 #> 2    theta2 0.1300866 0.007765928 0.1168075 0.1254525 0.1300085 0.1347168 #>       q97.5 #> 1 0.4124505 #> 2 0.1436619  draws <- sample(post, 10000) pairplot(draws, truth = theta_true)

title: Case study: inferring epidemic parameters (SIR)
text: posterior trustworthy calibrated. check Simulation-Based Calibration expected-coverage plot, neither needs reference posterior. plot chunk unnamed-chunk-5 plot chunk unnamed-chunk-5 rank histograms flat coverage curve hugs diagonal, posterior’s credible intervals mean say: 90% interval contains truth 90% time.
code: res <- sbc(fit, task$simulator, n_sbc = 80, n_posterior_samples = 300,            seed = 2) #> Warning in stats::chisq.test(tab): Chi-squared approximation may be incorrect #> Warning in stats::chisq.test(tab): Chi-squared approximation may be incorrect res                     # per-parameter uniformity p-values (large = good) #> <nsbi_sbc> 80 trials, 300 posterior samples each #>   per-parameter uniformity p-values (large = calibrated): #>     0.522  0.940  plot_sbc(res, param = 1)   # rank histogram: flat = calibrated plot_coverage(res)         # empirical vs nominal coverage: on the diagonal = good

title: Case study: inferring epidemic parameters (SIR)
text: Finally, push posterior draws back simulator compare predicted incidence curves observation. plot chunk unnamed-chunk-6 observed curve sit comfortably within cloud predictive draws. systematic mismatch flag model misspecification — signal point estimate can give .
code: pp <- posterior_predictive(post, task$simulator, n = 200) matplot(t(pp), type = "l", col = adjustcolor("grey", 0.3),         xlab = "observation time", ylab = "infected fraction") lines(as.numeric(x_obs), col = "firebrick", lwd = 2)

what: Spending simulations where they matter: sequential NPE
title: Case study: inferring epidemic parameters (SIR)
text: amortized fit spreads simulation budget whole prior, single outbreak interest, simulations describe epidemics nothing like observed one. Sequential NPE (npe_sequential(), using truncated proposals) alternates simulation training, restricting new round simulations parameter region current posterior considers plausible. plot chunk unnamed-chunk-7 comparable total simulation budget, sequential fit typically yields tighter posterior around particular outbreak. trade-: result specific x_obs, conditioning different incidence curve means refitting.
code: fit_seq <- npe_sequential(task$prior, task$simulator, x_obs = x_obs,                           n_rounds = 2, n_simulations = 1500,                           density_estimator = "mdn", max_epochs = 200, seed = 3) fit_seq                    # per-round budgets and acceptance rates #> <nsbi_snpe> Sequential NPE fit (TSNPE, truncated-prior proposals) #>   density estimator : mdn #>   rounds            : 2 #>   simulations       : 3000 #>   acceptance/round  : 1.00, 0.32 #>   targeted x_obs    : 0, 0, 0.004, 0.241, 0.139, 0.025, 0.004, 0.002, 0, 0 #>   NOT amortized: only valid at (or near) the targeted x_obs. #>   -> build a posterior with posterior(fit, x_obs = ...)  post_seq  <- posterior(fit_seq, x_obs = x_obs) draws_seq <- sample(post_seq, 10000) pairplot(draws_seq, truth = theta_true)

title: Case study: inferring epidemic parameters (SIR)
text: case study covered whole workflow: prior, simulator, amortized training, conditioning, calibration checks, predictive checks, sequential refinement. earlier vignettes treat stage depth — vignette("neuralsbi") core functions, vignette("density-estimators") use "maf" "nsf" instead MDN, vignette("diagnostics") complete set checks.

code: @Manual{,   title = {neuralsbi: Neural Simulation-Based Inference},   author = {Pedro {Nascimento de Lima}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.3},   url = {https://pedroliman.github.io/neuralsbi/}, }

title: Neural simulation-based inference in R
text: neuralsbi brings neural simulation-based inference (SBI) R. Given prior parameters simulator, trains neural network approximate Bayesian posterior p(theta | x): amortized, likelihood-free Bayesian inference, likelihood function Python. mirrors workflow Python sbi package, native R implementation: neural density estimators posterior estimation (mixture density networks, masked autoregressive flows, neural spline flows) run directly torch R package (libtorch), Python bridge. targets applied researchers rather ML engineers: familiar prior/simulator/posterior workflow, sensible defaults, built-posterior diagnostics: simulation-based calibration, expected coverage, TARP, posterior predictive checks.

title: Neural simulation-based inference in R
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("pedroliman/neuralsbi")  # the neural back end (once) install.packages("torch") torch::install_torch()

title: Neural simulation-based inference in R
text: Simulation-based inference fits posterior prior simulator, likelihood required. keep setup familiar, ordinary linear regression written simulator: response y scattered around line, y ~ Normal(alpha + beta * x, sigma) — model might write Stan. likelihood easy write , exactly makes good check: know coefficients generated data, can confirm posterior recovers . posterior mean recovers coefficients generated data:  posterior gives point estimate; calibration checks simulation-based calibration live vignette("diagnostics"). ’re interested sbi languages functionality available , see awesome neural SBI repo; good implementations python Julia.
code: library(neuralsbi) set.seed(1)  # Regression design: a single covariate x, measured at 50 fixed points. N <- 50 x <- seq(-1, 1, length.out = N)  # Simulator: given rows of (alpha, beta, sigma), draw one response vector y each # from y ~ Normal(alpha + beta * x, sigma). Fully vectorised over the rows of # theta, and it only generates data — no fitting happens here. simulator <- function(theta) {   alpha <- theta[, 1]   beta  <- theta[, 2]   sigma <- theta[, 3]   mu <- outer(beta, x) + alpha                       # row i is the line alpha_i + beta_i * x   mu + matrix(rnorm(length(mu)), nrow(mu)) * sigma   # add row-specific Gaussian noise }  # Priors over the intercept, slope, and noise scale, then train the posterior. prior <- prior_uniform(low = c(-3, -3, 0.1), high = c(3, 3, 2)) fit   <- npe(prior, simulator, n_simulations = 10000, seed = 1)  # Simulate one data set from known coefficients, then infer them back. The # observation the posterior conditions on is the response vector y. theta_true <- c(alpha = 2, beta = -1, sigma = 0.5) set.seed(38)                       # a fixed, representative data set y_obs      <- simulator(rbind(theta_true)) post       <- posterior(fit, x_obs = y_obs) draws      <- sample(post, 10000) rbind(truth = theta_true, posterior_mean = colMeans(draws)) #>                   alpha      beta     sigma #> truth          2.000000 -1.000000 0.5000000 #> posterior_mean 2.003668 -1.041009 0.5003288 pairplot(draws, truth = theta_true, labels = c("alpha", "beta", "sigma")) map_estimate(post)     # posterior mode #> [1]  1.9989619 -1.0442036  0.4702335

title: Neural simulation-based inference in R
text: package website four vignettes build : Getting started — core prior/simulator/posterior workflow. Choosing density estimator — MDN, MAF, NSF, torch-free baseline. Checking posterior — calibration predictive diagnostics. Case study: SIR epidemic model — full Bayesian workflow applied problem.

title: Neural simulation-based inference in R

what: Build the embedding torch submodule, or NULL for the identity embedding. — build_embedding_module
title: Build the embedding torch submodule, or NULL for the identity embedding. — build_embedding_module
text: Constructed lazily torch object exists package-load time. Returns instantiated nn_module (call site stores submodule parameters train jointly travel estimator's state_dict).

title: Build the embedding torch submodule, or NULL for the identity embedding. — build_embedding_module
code: build_embedding_module(spec, dim_x)

title: Classifier two-sample test (C2ST) — c2st
text: Trains logistic-regression classifier distinguish samples x samples y using cross-validation. test accuracy near 0.5 means two sample sets indistinguishable (good); near 1.0 means differ. standard SBI metric comparing estimated posterior reference (e.g. analytic posterior long-run MCMC draws).

title: Classifier two-sample test (C2ST) — c2st
code: c2st(x, y, n_folds = 5L, seed = NULL)

title: Classifier two-sample test (C2ST) — c2st
text: x, y Matrices samples (rows = draws, cols = dimensions). n_folds Number cross-validation folds. seed Optional seed.

title: Classifier two-sample test (C2ST) — c2st
text: list mean CV accuracy per-fold accuracies.

title: Conditional density estimators — density_estimator
text: conditional density estimator learns \(q_\phi(\theta \mid x)\). neuralsbi every estimator trained standardized space exposes two generics:

title: Conditional density estimators — density_estimator
text: de_log_prob(de, theta, x) – log density theta given x de_sample(de, x, n) – draw n parameter vectors given single x Two estimators ship today: "mdn" – Mixture Density Network (neural network -> Gaussian mixture), workhorse, requires torch back end. "linear_gaussian" – closed-form conditional Gaussian baseline (least-squares mean, residual covariance). neural network, torch. exact linear-Gaussian simulators doubles fast baseline regression-test oracle.

title: Posterior diagnostics — diagnostics
text: Tools check whether trained posterior trustworthy:

title: Posterior diagnostics — diagnostics
text: sbc() – Simulation-Based Calibration rank statistics expected_coverage() – nominal vs. empirical credible-interval coverage c2st() – classifier two-sample test two sample sets posterior_predictive() – draw data fitted posterior

what: Multivariate normal log density using a precomputed upper-Cholesky factor (R such that Sigma = t(R) %*% R, i.e. chol(Sigma)). — dmvnorm_chol
title: Multivariate normal log density using a precomputed upper-Cholesky factor (R such that Sigma = t(R) %*% R, i.e. chol(Sigma)). — dmvnorm_chol
text: Multivariate normal log density using precomputed upper-Cholesky factor (R Sigma = t(R) %*% R, .e. chol(Sigma)).

title: Multivariate normal log density using a precomputed upper-Cholesky factor (R such that Sigma = t(R) %*% R, i.e. chol(Sigma)). — dmvnorm_chol
code: dmvnorm_chol(x, mean, R, log = TRUE)

what: Apply an estimator's embedding to conditioning data, if it has one. — embed_x
title: Apply an estimator's embedding to conditioning data, if it has one. — embed_x
text: Estimators call per forward/inverse pass embedding runs single time; raw (standardized) x still enters de_* boundary, keeping de$dim_x raw data dimension.

title: Apply an estimator's embedding to conditioning data, if it has one. — embed_x
code: embed_x(net, x)

what: Embedding (summary) networks for structured observations — embedding_mlp
title: Embedding (summary) networks for structured observations — embedding_mlp
text: Raw observations often high-dimensional structured (time series, set summary statistics, image) feeding x straight density estimator wastes capacity. embedding network learns low- dimensional summary \(h = f_\psi(x)\) jointly density estimator, conditioning path becomes \(q_\phi(\theta \mid f_\psi(x))\). mirrors sbi's embedding_net argument.

title: Embedding (summary) networks for structured observations — embedding_mlp
code: embedding_mlp(output_dim = 16L, hidden = c(64L, 64L))

title: Embedding (summary) networks for structured observations — embedding_mlp
text: output_dim Number summary features network emits. effective data dimension density estimator conditions . hidden Integer vector hidden-layer widths (ReLU layers). empty vector gives single linear map output_dim.

title: Embedding (summary) networks for structured observations — embedding_mlp
text: nsbi_embedding specification. carries torch objects (network built lazily fit time), safe construct without torch installed.

title: Embedding (summary) networks for structured observations — embedding_mlp
text: embedding_mlp() builds multilayer-perceptron summary network: stack fully connected ReLU layers mapping (standardized) data vector output_dim features. Pass result npe() via embedding_net; trained end end estimator parameters live inside fitted network, sampling log_prob route automatically. embedding consumes standardized data (z-scoring npe() applies x without embedding), keeps summary network's inputs common scale. Standardization features intentionally left network ; estimators operate raw embedding output.

title: Embedding (summary) networks for structured observations — embedding_mlp
code: emb <- embedding_mlp(output_dim = 8, hidden = c(64, 64)) # fit <- npe(prior, simulator, density_estimator = "maf", embedding_net = emb)

what: Effective conditioning dimension after an (optional) embedding. — embedding_output_dim
title: Effective conditioning dimension after an (optional) embedding. — embedding_output_dim
text: identity embedding (spec = NULL) leaves data dimension unchanged; otherwise estimator conditions output_dim features.

title: Effective conditioning dimension after an (optional) embedding. — embedding_output_dim

title: Expected coverage of central credible intervals — expected_coverage

title: Expected coverage of central credible intervals — expected_coverage
text: sbc_result nsbi_sbc object sbc(). levels Nominal credibility levels evaluate.

title: Expected coverage of central credible intervals — expected_coverage

title: Posterior log-density — log_prob
text: post nsbi_posterior object. theta Matrix (vector) parameter values evaluate. x Observation condition (defaults x_obs). normalize bounded priors, renormalize estimated acceptance probability return -Inf outside prior support. n_normalization Number draws used estimate normalizing (acceptance) constant normalize = TRUE.

what: MADE masks for one autoregressive transform. — made_masks
title: MADE masks for one autoregressive transform. — made_masks
text: Degrees: theta inputs get 1..p, conditioning inputs get 0 (visible ), hidden units cycle 1..(p-1) (0 p = 1). connection hidden unit requires hidden_degree >= input_degree; connection output dimension d requires d > hidden_degree. makes output d function \(\theta_{<d}\) x .

what: Masked Autoregressive Flow (MAF) conditional density estimator — maf
title: Masked Autoregressive Flow (MAF) conditional density estimator — maf
text: normalizing flow maps parameters \(\theta\) standard-normal base variable stack invertible transforms, giving exact densities change variables. MAF (Papamakarios et al., 2017) uses masked autoregressive networks (MADE, Germain et al., 2015): transform $$u_d = (\theta_d - \mu_d(\theta_{<d}, x)) \exp(-\alpha_d(\theta_{<d}, x)),$$ masks guarantee \(\mu_d, \alpha_d\) depend earlier dimensions \(\theta\) (freely conditioning data x). Density evaluation single forward pass; sampling inverts transform one dimension time. transforms parameter order reversed every dimension gets conditioned every across stack.

title: Masked Autoregressive Flow (MAF) conditional density estimator — maf
text: sbi's default flow family, default estimator neuralsbi . handles non-Gaussian posteriors MDN struggles . selected default, explicitly npe(..., density_estimator = "maf").

what: Mixture Density Network (MDN) conditional density estimator — mdn
title: Mixture Density Network (MDN) conditional density estimator — mdn
text: MDN one neural density estimators neuralsbi (default MAF, matching Python sbi). multilayer perceptron maps data x parameters Gaussian mixture parameters \(\theta\): mixture logits, component means, (full) lower-triangular Cholesky factors component covariance. Training minimizes negative log-likelihood \(\theta\) mixture, – simulations drawn prior – yields direct amortized approximation posterior \(p(\theta \mid x)\).

title: Mixture Density Network (MDN) conditional density estimator — mdn
text: native R/torch implementation multivariate-Gaussian mixture density network (Bishop, 1994).

title: neuralsbi: Neural Simulation-Based Inference — neuralsbi-package
text: native R implementation neural simulation-based inference, focused Neural Posterior Estimation. Given prior parameters simulator, 'neuralsbi' trains conditional neural density estimator approximate Bayesian posterior, enabling amortized, likelihood-free inference. Neural estimators run 'torch' back end, Python dependency. targets applied researchers want approachable interface sensible defaults built-posterior diagnostics.

title: neuralsbi: Neural Simulation-Based Inference — neuralsbi-package
text: Maintainer: Pedro Nascimento de Lima plima@rand.org (ORCID) Authors: Pedro Nascimento de Lima plima@rand.org (ORCID)

title: Neural Posterior Estimation (NPE) — npe
text: npe() main entry point. Given prior either simulator (call) set pre-computed simulations (theta, x), trains conditional density estimator whose output directly approximates posterior \(p(\theta \mid x)\). single-round, amortized NPE: training , can condition observation without re-simulating.

title: Neural Posterior Estimation (NPE) — npe
code: npe(   prior,   simulator = NULL,   n_simulations = 1000,   theta = NULL,   x = NULL,   density_estimator = c("maf", "mdn", "nsf", "linear_gaussian"),   n_components = 10L,   n_transforms = 5L,   hidden = c(50L, 50L),   embedding_net = NULL,   max_epochs = 2000L,   batch_size = 200L,   lr = 5e-04,   validation_fraction = 0.1,   patience = 20L,   n_restarts = 1L,   clip_grad_norm = 5,   standardize = TRUE,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Neural Posterior Estimation (NPE) — npe
text: prior nsbi_prior (see prior_uniform(), prior_normal()). simulator function mapping n x dim matrix parameters n x d matrix simulated data. Ignored theta x given. n_simulations Number prior draws simulate simulator used theta/x supplied. theta, x Optional pre-computed simulations. supplied, simulator n_simulations ignored. density_estimator One "maf" (Masked Autoregressive Flow, needs torch; default, matching Python sbi), "mdn" (neural Mixture Density Network, needs torch), "nsf" (Neural Spline Flow, needs torch), "linear_gaussian" (closed-form baseline, torch), function function(theta, x) returning fitted estimator. n_components, hidden MDN settings: number mixture components (default 10, sbi) vector hidden-layer widths. n_transforms MAF/NSF setting: number stacked autoregressive transforms (default 5, sbi). embedding_net Optional summary network built embedding_mlp(). supplied, neural estimators condition learned features \(f_\psi(x)\) instead raw data, training embedding jointly. Ignored (warning) "linear_gaussian". max_epochs, batch_size, lr, validation_fraction, patience Neural training controls (Adam optimizer, early stopping validation loss). defaults (batch_size = 200, lr = 5e-4, validation_fraction = 0.1, patience = 20) match Python sbi; max_epochs high guard cap early stopping normally reaches first. n_restarts Train many independently initialized networks keep one best validation loss (guards bad initializations MDN mode collapse). clip_grad_norm Maximum gradient norm training (Inf disables clipping). learning rate also decays 2x 10 epochs without validation improvement. standardize Whether z-score theta x training (strongly recommended; default TRUE). seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. verbose Print training progress. ... Passed density estimator.

title: Neural Posterior Estimation (NPE) — npe
text: object class nsbi_npe. Turn usable posterior posterior(), sample directly sample().

title: Neural Posterior Estimation (NPE) — npe
code: prior <- prior_uniform(c(-2, -2, -2), c(2, 2, 2)) simulator <- function(theta) theta + 1 + matrix(rnorm(length(theta), sd = 0.1),                                                  nrow = nrow(theta)) fit <- npe(prior, simulator, n_simulations = 2000,            density_estimator = "linear_gaussian") post <- posterior(fit, x_obs = c(0.8, 0.6, 0.4)) draws <- sample(post, 1000)

what: Sequential NPE with truncated-prior proposals (TSNPE) — npe_sequential
title: Sequential NPE with truncated-prior proposals (TSNPE) — npe_sequential
text: Multi-round NPE targeting single observation x_obs. Single-round npe() spends simulation budget across whole prior; one observation matters, simulations land regions posterior never visits. npe_sequential() implements truncated sequential NPE (TSNPE, Deistler et al. 2022): round prior truncated highest-probability region current posterior estimate, next round's parameters drawn truncated prior. every proposal proportional prior support, standard NPE loss stays valid – importance-weight atomic correction needed, makes TSNPE simplest correct sequential scheme.

title: Sequential NPE with truncated-prior proposals (TSNPE) — npe_sequential
code: npe_sequential(   prior,   simulator,   x_obs,   n_rounds = 2L,   n_simulations = 1000L,   density_estimator = c("maf", "mdn", "nsf", "linear_gaussian"),   epsilon = 1e-04,   n_truncation_samples = 5000L,   max_proposal_batches = 200L,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Sequential NPE with truncated-prior proposals (TSNPE) — npe_sequential
text: prior nsbi_prior (see prior_uniform(), prior_normal()). simulator function mapping n x dim matrix parameters n x d matrix simulated data. x_obs observation target. Sequential inference concentrates simulations around posterior observation. n_rounds Number rounds. Round 1 ordinary single-round NPE. n_simulations Simulation budget per round; either scalar vector length n_rounds. density_estimator Passed npe() round. epsilon Mass cut truncation: proposal region 1 - epsilon highest-probability region current posterior. n_truncation_samples Posterior draws used locate truncation threshold round. max_proposal_batches Cap rejection-sampling batches per round. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. verbose Print per-round progress. ... Passed npe() (estimator training settings).

title: Sequential NPE with truncated-prior proposals (TSNPE) — npe_sequential
text: object class c("nsbi_snpe", "nsbi_npe") rounds field recording per-round budgets, acceptance rates, thresholds.

title: Sequential NPE with truncated-prior proposals (TSNPE) — npe_sequential
text: rounds accumulate: round's estimator trained simulations far. final fit returned nsbi_npe (subclass nsbi_snpe) works posterior(), sample() diagnostics, unlike single-round NPE amortized: trustworthy (near) x_obs. Proposal draws obtained rejection: prior candidates kept posterior log-density clears epsilon-quantile threshold current posterior's draws. posterior much narrower prior acceptance rate falls; round stops max_proposal_batches batches continues draws , warning.

title: Sequential NPE with truncated-prior proposals (TSNPE) — npe_sequential
text: Deistler, Goncalves & Macke (2022), "Truncated proposals scalable hassle-free simulation-based inference", NeurIPS. doi:10.48550/arXiv.2210.04815

title: Sequential NPE with truncated-prior proposals (TSNPE) — npe_sequential
code: prior <- prior_normal(mean = c(0, 0), sd = 1) simulator <- function(theta) theta + matrix(rnorm(length(theta), sd = 0.3),                                             nrow = nrow(theta)) fit <- npe_sequential(prior, simulator, x_obs = c(0.5, -0.5),                       n_rounds = 2, n_simulations = 1000,                       density_estimator = "linear_gaussian") post <- posterior(fit, x_obs = c(0.5, -0.5)) draws <- sample(post, 1000)

what: Neural Spline Flow (NSF) conditional density estimator — nsf
title: Neural Spline Flow (NSF) conditional density estimator — nsf
text: autoregressive flow whose per-dimension transform monotonic rational-quadratic spline (Durkan et al., 2019) instead MAF's affine shift--scale. Splines far expressive per layer, helps sharply non-Gaussian posteriors (SLCP, two moons). reuse MADE masking machinery R/flows.R; MADE outputs 3K - 1 spline parameters per dimension (K bin widths, K bin heights, K - 1 interior derivatives). spline acts [-B, B] identity outside (linear tails), standard-normal base distribution unaffected tails. Note: NSF implementations elsewhere often use coupling layers; autoregressive — density family, different conditioning structure.

title: Neural Spline Flow (NSF) conditional density estimator — nsf
text: Select npe(..., density_estimator = "nsf").

title: Standardization (z-scoring) helpers — standardize
text: Neural density estimators train far reliably inputs targets standardized roughly zero mean unit variance. neuralsbi learns transforms training simulations, applies internally, inverts returning posterior draws / densities.

what: Log absolute Jacobian determinant of the inverse standardization (standardized -> original). Constant, so a scalar. — standardizer_log_jac
title: Log absolute Jacobian determinant of the inverse standardization (standardized -> original). Constant, so a scalar. — standardizer_log_jac
text: Log absolute Jacobian determinant inverse standardization (standardized -> original). Constant, scalar.

title: Log absolute Jacobian determinant of the inverse standardization (standardized -> original). Constant, so a scalar. — standardizer_log_jac
code: standardizer_log_jac(std)

title: Summaries and tidy accessors — summaries
text: summary() methods fits, posteriors, samples, plus .data.frame() posterior draws results drop straight data-frame workflows (dplyr, ggplot2, ...).

title: Summaries and tidy accessors — summaries
code: # S3 method for class 'nsbi_samples' as.data.frame(x, row.names = NULL, optional = FALSE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'nsbi_samples' summary(object, probs = c(0.025, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.975), ...)  # S3 method for class 'nsbi_posterior' summary(object, n = 1000L, x = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'nsbi_npe' summary(object, ...)

title: Summaries and tidy accessors — summaries
text: x Observation condition (defaults posterior's x_obs); .data.frame(), samples object. row.names, optional Standard .data.frame() arguments. ... Additional arguments passed methods. object nsbi_samples matrix sample(), nsbi_posterior, nsbi_npe fit. probs Quantiles report. n Number draws used summarize posterior.

title: Summaries and tidy accessors — summaries
text: samples posteriors, data frame one row per parameter (mean, sd, quantiles). fits, invisible list training metadata.

title: TARP expected coverage — tarp
text: Tests Accuracy Random Points (Lemos et al. 2023). trial true parameter drawn prior, data simulated, posterior samples drawn conditioned data. Given random reference point, fraction posterior samples closer reference truth credibility level smallest distance-based credible region contains truth. calibrated posterior fractions uniform, expected coverage probability (ECP) credibility level alpha equals alpha.

title: TARP expected coverage — tarp
code: tarp(   fit,   simulator,   prior = fit$prior,   n_tarp = 200L,   n_posterior_samples = 1000L,   references = c("uniform", "prior"),   seed = NULL )

title: TARP expected coverage — tarp
text: fit nsbi_npe fit (amortized posterior). simulator simulator used inference. prior prior used inference (defaults fit$prior). n_tarp Number TARP trials (fresh (theta, x) pairs). n_posterior_samples Posterior draws per trial. references draw reference points: "uniform" (default, uniform hyper-rectangle spanned true parameter draws, paper) "prior" (draws prior). seed Optional seed.

title: TARP expected coverage — tarp
text: object class nsbi_tarp per-trial coverage values ECP curve. Plot plot_tarp().

title: TARP expected coverage — tarp
text: Unlike sbc(), ranks parameter marginally, TARP joint test: can detect posteriors whose marginals calibrated whose correlation structure wrong. Distances computed z-scoring parameter (using spread true draws), parameters different scales contribute comparably.

title: TARP expected coverage — tarp
text: Lemos, Coogan, Hezaveh & Perreault-Levasseur (2023), "Sampling-based accuracy testing posterior estimators general inference", ICML. doi:10.48550/arXiv.2302.03026

title: Benchmark tasks — tasks
text: Standard simulation-based-inference benchmark tasks, following definitions sbibm benchmark suite. task bundles prior, simulator, (one exists) analytic reference posterior, object drives unit tests, calibration studies, benchmark harness inst/benchmarks/. task_gaussian_linear() – conjugate Gaussian; analytic posterior. task_two_moons() – crescent-shaped, bimodal posterior. task_slcp() – 5 parameters, 8-dimensional data, strongly   non-Gaussian posterior. task_sir() – SIR epidemic dynamics log-normal priors;   closed-form posterior (verify SBC / coverage).

title: Benchmark tasks — tasks
code: task_gaussian_linear(dim = 10L, prior_var = 0.1, noise_var = 0.1)  task_two_moons()  task_slcp()  task_sir(N = 1e6, days = 160, n_points = 10L, n_obs_draws = 1000L)

title: Benchmark tasks — tasks
text: dim Parameter/data dimension Gaussian linear task (default 10). prior_var, noise_var Prior likelihood variances Gaussian linear task. N, days, n_points, n_obs_draws SIR task: population size, horizon days, number observation times, binomial trials per observation.

title: Benchmark tasks — tasks
text: list class nsbi_task elements name, prior, simulator, dim_theta, dim_x, optionally reference_posterior(x_obs, n) returning exact posterior draws.

what: Shared training engine for torch conditional density estimators — train_conditional_de
title: Shared training engine for torch conditional density estimators — train_conditional_de
text: neural estimators (MDN, MAF, NSF) share one training loop robustness features implemented : train/validation split, Adam, minibatching, early stopping validation loss, learning-rate decay plateau, gradient clipping, best--n_restarts reinitialization. defaults (batch 200, lr 5e-4, 10% validation, patience 20, clip norm 5) match Python sbi, results comparable across two packages.

title: Shared training engine for torch conditional density estimators — train_conditional_de
code: train_conditional_de(   build_net,   log_prob_fn,   theta,   x,   max_epochs = 2000L,   batch_size = 200L,   lr = 5e-04,   validation_fraction = 0.1,   patience = 20L,   n_restarts = 1L,   clip_grad_norm = 5,   lr_patience = 10L,   lr_factor = 0.5,   min_lr = 1e-06,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Shared training engine for torch conditional density estimators — train_conditional_de
text: build_net zero-argument function returning fresh torch module. Called per restart restart gets new initial weights. log_prob_fn function(net, theta, x) returning length-b tensor log densities (b, p) theta tensor (b, q) x tensor. theta, x Standardized training matrices. n_restarts Train many independently initialized networks keep one best validation loss. clip_grad_norm Maximum gradient norm (set Inf disable). lr_patience, lr_factor, min_lr Reduce learning rate lr_factor lr_patience epochs without validation improvement, min_lr.

title: Shared training engine for torch conditional density estimators — train_conditional_de
text: list(net, best_val_loss, history) history data frame per-epoch train/validation losses winning restart.

title: neuralsbi 0.3.2
text: README now generated README.Rmd, usage example runs render time output drift code. example plain linear regression: posterior recovers ground-truth coefficients, reader can check ordinary least squares.

title: neuralsbi 0.3.1
text: CRAN resubmission fixes. Routed summary() .data.frame() methods single summaries help topic (drifted separate .Rd files duplicated \alias entries, also produced duplicate HTML anchors). Wrapped theta_{<d} \eqn{} made_masks docs Rd longer drops braces. Dropped bare “NPE” acronym DESCRIPTION avoid spurious misspelling note.

title: neuralsbi 0.3.0
text: Defaults now match Python sbi, workflow reads packages results can cross-checked. Changes npe() defaults: density estimator now "maf" ("mdn"); MDN mixture components default 10 (5); NSF spline bins default 10 (8); training batch size 200 (100). max_epochs raised 2000 guard cap early stopping (patience = 20) normally reaches first, mirroring sbi’s effectively-unbounded epoch budget. lr, validation_fraction, patience, clip_grad_norm, n_transforms, hidden already matched. Pass explicitly recover previous behavior. First CRAN submission. Dropped development .9000 version suffix, removed redundant Author/Maintainer fields (now derived Authors@R), tidied package title. Embedding networks (roadmap v0.4). embedding_mlp() builds learned summary network maps raw observations low-dimensional feature vector; pass npe(..., embedding_net = ) MDN, MAF, NSF estimators condition features instead raw data, training embedding jointly. estimators still take raw x de_* boundary (dim_x unchanged), sampling log_prob route embedding automatically. Ignored, warning, linear_gaussian.

title: neuralsbi 0.2.4.9000 (development)
text: Vignettes now show real output. precomputed: vignette’s evaluated source lives vignettes/<name>.Rmd.orig, vignettes/precompute.R bakes static vignettes/<name>.Rmd (results, printed values, figures inlined). CI pkgdown re-render static Markdown torch build time, expensive neural training runs , locally, instead every build. Re-run Rscript vignettes/precompute.R editing .Rmd.orig. Two-moons calibration study (inst/benchmarks/two_moons_calibration.R): SBC, expected coverage, TARP two-moons NSF fit, figures written docs/figures/ (roadmap milestone M2).

title: neuralsbi 0.2.3.9000
text: Package website built pkgdown, deployed CI https://pedroliman.github.io/neuralsbi/. Four vignettes build : getting started, choosing density estimator, checking posterior, SIR case study (now also demonstrates npe_sequential()). Removed truncated duplicate SIR vignette. README rewritten standard terse form; authorship recorded DESCRIPTION (Pedro Nascimento de Lima, ORCID).

title: neuralsbi 0.2.2.9000
text: New npe_sequential(): multi-round NPE targeting single observation via truncated-prior proposals (TSNPE, Deistler et al. 2022). round truncates prior highest-probability region current posterior retrains accumulated simulations; standard NPE loss stays valid, importance correction needed. Returns nsbi_snpe fit works posterior(), sample(), diagnostics, valid targeted x_obs. Verified analytic linear-Gaussian posterior.

title: neuralsbi 0.2.1.9000
text: New tarp() diagnostic plot_tarp() (Lemos et al. 2023): joint expected-coverage test using random reference points, complementing per-parameter sbc() ranks. Detects posteriors calibrated marginals wrong correlation structure. New plot_posterior_predictive(): marginal predictive histograms observation marked; returns observation’s predictive quantiles. Leakage correction now test: bounded prior, renormalized log_prob() integrates one support returns -Inf outside (test-posterior-normalization.R). Fixed CI. R CMD check failed three counts: npe() example required libtorch (now uses torch-free linear_gaussian estimator runs unconditionally), hand-maintained npe.Rd/fit_mdn.Rd usage sections drifted behind code (missing n_restarts, clip_grad_norm, n_transforms, "maf"/"nsf" options), CLAUDE.md .Rbuildignore. test-torch job also failed torch 0.17 refuses TORCH_HOME exist; workflow now creates first.

title: neuralsbi 0.2.0.9000
text: Shared training engine neural estimators (train_conditional_de()): best--n restarts, learning-rate decay plateau, gradient clipping, per-epoch loss history. Masked Autoregressive Flow (density_estimator = "maf") Neural Spline Flow ("nsf", autoregressive rational-quadratic splines) join MDN closed-form linear_gaussian baseline. Benchmark tasks (task_gaussian_linear(), task_two_moons(), task_slcp(), task_sir()) shared tests inst/benchmarks/ head--head benchmark harness. summary() methods, .data.frame() tidy accessor, plot_coverage(). SIR applied case-study vignette. CI: R CMD check plus test-torch job cached libtorch.

title: neuralsbi 0.1.0
text: First pilot release: priors, single-round amortized npe(), linear_gaussian MDN estimators, posterior sampling leakage correction, SBC, expected coverage, C2ST, posterior-predictive checks, pairplot(), plot_sbc().
